root_text.sn17.2:1.2: “Dāruṇo, bhikkhave, lābhasakkārasiloko kaṭuko pharuso antarāyiko anuttarassa yogakkhemassa adhigamāya.
root_text.sn17.2:1.3: Seyyathāpi, bhikkhave, bāḷisiko āmisagataṁ baḷisaṁ gambhīre udakarahade pakkhipeyya.
root_text.sn17.2:1.5: Evañhi so, bhikkhave, maccho gilabaḷiso bāḷisikassa anayaṁ āpanno byasanaṁ āpanno yathākāmakaraṇīyo bāḷisikassa.
root_text.sn17.2:2.1: Bāḷisikoti kho, bhikkhave, mārassetaṁ pāpimato adhivacanaṁ.
root_text.sn17.2:2.3: Yo hi koci, bhikkhave, bhikkhu uppannaṁ lābhasakkārasilokaṁ assādeti nikāmeti, ayaṁ vuccati, bhikkhave, bhikkhu gilabaḷiso mārassa anayaṁ āpanno byasanaṁ āpanno yathākāmakaraṇīyo pāpimato.
root_text.sn17.2:2.4: Evaṁ dāruṇo kho, bhikkhave, lābhasakkārasiloko kaṭuko pharuso antarāyiko anuttarassa yogakkhemassa adhigamāya.
root_text.sn17.2:2.6: ‘uppannaṁ lābhasakkārasilokaṁ pajahissāma, na ca no uppanno lābhasakkārasiloko cittaṁ pariyādāya ṭhassatī’ti.

translation_text.sn17.2:1.2: “Possessions, honor, and popularity are grim, bitter, and harsh. They’re an obstacle to reaching the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.sn17.2:1.3: Suppose an angler was to cast a baited hook into a deep lake.
translation_text.sn17.2:1.4: Seeing the bait, a fish would swallow it.
translation_text.sn17.2:1.5: And so the fish that swallowed the hook would meet with tragedy and disaster, and the angler can do what he wants with it.
translation_text.sn17.2:2.1: ‘Angler’ is a term for Māra the Wicked.
translation_text.sn17.2:2.2: ‘Hook’ is a term for possessions, honor, and popularity.
translation_text.sn17.2:2.3: Whoever enjoys and likes arisen possessions, honor, and popularity is called a mendicant who has swallowed Māra’s hook. They’ve met with tragedy and disaster, and the Wicked One can do with them what he wants.
translation_text.sn17.2:2.4: So grim are possessions, honor, and popularity—bitter and harsh, an obstacle to reaching the supreme sanctuary from the yoke.
translation_text.sn17.2:2.5: So you should train like this:
translation_text.sn17.2:2.6: ‘We will give up arisen possessions, honor, and popularity, and we won’t let them occupy our minds.’
